mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL directly increases inflation expectations, prompting market repricing of equity valuations through higher discount rates, which mechanically reduces S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL increases.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 4.2%, labor market slack triggers immediate corporate earnings downgrade expectations, causing institutional portfolio rebalancing that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >55% of triggered months, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in RSAFS and HOUST signals robust consumer spending and housing market strength, which compounds corporate revenue growth expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day forward returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a decline in INDPRO indicates supply-side contraction that outpaces demand destruction, forcing equity markets to price in recessionary earnings cuts and producing negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive 20-day returns in >58% of instances, or regime interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in PAYEMS reflects expanding employment capacity and rising aggregate income, which directly supports consumer spending and corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 5-day forward returns, or Rank IC falls below -0.02 over any 24-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, borrowing costs for corporations and households rise sharply, compressing profit margins and reducing capital expenditure, which mechanically forces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >62% of months where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where GDPC1 growth outpaces CPIAUCSL indicates real economic expansion without inflationary pressure, allowing the Federal Reserve to maintain accommodative policy and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth outpacing inflation yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of quarters, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit (AIC/BIC).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, rising INDPRO signals rapid capacity utilization restoration that precedes earnings revisions, triggering algorithmic momentum buying and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases produce negative 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the signal reverses direction when volatility exceeds 20% annualized.
